title: Encoding Stewardship and Stability
subtitle: Technical coherence, computational validity, linguistic judgment, practical finality, and forkability
summary: A governance note explaining that FSW/SWU are stable author-stewarded encodings for computational validity, not claims of linguistic or community authority.
synopsis: Formal SignWriting is author-stewarded infrastructure. Steve Slevinski stewards the formal encoding he authored and maintains: the supported FSW/SWU path, its technical coherence, stability, documentation, compatibility, and reference behavior. That stewardship defines well-formed structures for parsing, rendering, search, sorting, and interchange. It does not decide good spelling, local preference, reader judgment, community approval, or AI deployment. The supported Sutton SignWriting production path remains the established FSW/SWU model because it has reached practical production maturity, while open licensing permits use, critique, rejection, and forks.

slides.title: Core Boundary
slides.summary: Formal SignWriting defines well-formed structures for computation, not good or approved spelling for readers.

image_prompts.concrete: Technical governance slide separating computer validity from reader judgment, with FSW and SWU at the stable center
image_prompts.abstract: Two clearly separated columns: well formed for software and well written for readers

slides.speech: The central boundary is simple. Formal SignWriting defines what is well-formed for software. It does not decide what is well-written for the reader.

slides.title: What Encoding Stewardship Covers
slides.summary: Encoding stewardship covers grammar, valid structure, parsing, rendering, search, sorting, interchange, and compatibility.

image_prompts.concrete: Formal SignWriting encoding map showing grammar, parsing, rendering, search, sorting, interchange, and compatibility connected to one stable model
image_prompts.abstract: A technical hub with stable spokes to software functions

slides.speech: Encoding stewardship is real, but it is bounded. It covers the formal model: grammar, valid structures, parsing, rendering, search, sorting, interchange, and compatibility with the established production path.
slides.notes: Make the stewardship responsibility concrete before narrowing it.

slides.title: What It Does Not Cover
slides.summary: The encoding does not decide spelling quality, local preference, community approval, corpus policy, media consent, or AI safety.

image_prompts.concrete: Boundary slide showing spelling, community norms, corpus governance, signer media consent, and AI deployment outside the encoding box
image_prompts.abstract: Responsibility zones outside a stable encoding boundary

slides.speech: Formal SignWriting does not decide whether a spelling is good, preferred, local, standard, poetic, readable, or community-approved. It also does not decide corpus governance, media consent, dataset permissions, or AI deployment safety.

slides.title: Stable Supported Path
slides.summary: FSW and SWU are stable, mature, and no longer under ordinary design revision as the supported Sutton SignWriting production encoding.

image_prompts.concrete: Stable software road map with FSW and SWU as the supported production path and optional alternate paths branching outward
image_prompts.abstract: A firm central path with optional branches

slides.speech: Finality here means stable, mature, and no longer under ordinary design revision. It is a stewardship judgment, not a refusal of critique. Future work concerns documentation, tooling, preservation, datasets, access, research use, and bridge support, not redesign of the core model.
slides.notes: Use practical finality rather than absolute finality.

slides.title: Voluntary And Forkable
slides.summary: Open licensing permits use, critique, rejection, alternatives, and forks while preserving a clear supported model.

image_prompts.concrete: Open infrastructure slide showing use, study, critique, rejection, alternative implementations, and forks around a maintained supported path
image_prompts.abstract: Open branches around a stable maintained center

slides.speech: The supported path does not compel adoption. Communities, institutions, researchers, and developers may use it, ignore it, critique it, reject it, build alternatives, or fork it under the applicable licenses.
slides.notes: Close by showing the model is stable without being coercive.
